ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general glutinous rice ball entry; no specific peanut-filled tang yuan in longan soup variant exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific tang yuan code. A0CKP (rice-based sweet snack) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for tang yuan or glutinous rice ball dessert.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 glutinous rice balls (approximately 120 g) served in approximately 100 g of longan red date soup, totalling about 220 g.

nutrient_source.title: Tang Yuan (Glutinous Rice Balls with Peanut Filling) — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA FoodData Central Glutinous Rice and Peanut component data
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Foodo entry for peanut tang yuan in longan red date soup was found. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis: glutinous rice flour dough (~55% by weight), peanut-sugar filling (~20% by weight), and sweet longan red date soup (~25% by weight). Reference values drawn from HPB Singapore nutrient data for glutinous rice products and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for peanuts (NDB 16087), glutinous rice (NDB 20060), longan (NDB 09164), and red dates/jujube (NDB 09420). Energy calculated using Atwater factors.

gi_source.title: Glutinous rice-based products GI — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for peanut tang yuan in longan red date soup. GI estimated at 65 based on glutinous rice-based desserts in the University of Sydney GI Database (glutinous rice GI range 86–98 for plain cooked; however, when formed into filled balls with peanut fat and sugar filling and served in sweet soup, the fat and protein content moderates glycemic response). Analogous reference: sweet glutinous rice products with fat-containing fillings have reported GI values in the 60–70 range. GL per serving calculated as (GI x carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 x 38.0) / 100 = 24.7, using 38.0 g available carbohydrate per 220 g serving.

hypertension.guidance: With approximately 28 mg sodium per 100 g, peanut tang yuan in longan red date soup is low in sodium and is generally suitable for individuals managing hypertension when consumed in standard single-serving portions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load and refined carbohydrate content from glutinous rice flour and added sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a medium GI of approximately 65 and a glycemic load of ~24.7 per serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size or avoid this dish, as the glutinous rice flour base and added sugar in both the filling and sweet soup can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and zero cholesterol; peanut filling provides unsaturated fats
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Peanut tang yuan is low in saturated fat (~0.9 g per 100 g) and contains no cholesterol; the peanut filling contributes beneficial monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats, making this dessert relatively heart-friendly when consumed occasionally and in standard portions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Peanut Glutinous Rice Ball Tang Yuan (3 pc) in Longan Red Date Soup is a traditional Chinese dessert widely enjoyed in Singapore and across the Chinese diaspora, particularly during the Lantern Festival (Yuan Xiao Jie) and Winter Solstice (Dong Zhi). The dish consists of three round, chewy glutinous rice flour dumplings filled with a sweet, coarsely ground peanut and sugar mixture, served in a warm, fragrant sweet soup made from dried longan (gui yuan) and red dates (hong zao / jujube). The outer skin is made from glutinous rice flour (nuo mi fen) kneaded with water into a smooth, pliable dough, then hand-rolled and filled before being boiled until they float. The peanut filling provides a slightly gritty, nutty sweetness that contrasts with the smooth, elastic skin. The longan red date soup is naturally sweet, lightly floral, and warming, with the red dates contributing a mild tannin note. Nutritionally, this dessert is moderate in energy (~198 kcal per 100 g), relatively low in protein (~3.8 g) and fat (~5.2 g), and high in carbohydrates (~34.5 g), primarily from glutinous rice starch and added sugars. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), and the glycemic load per standard 3-piece serving is approximately 24.7, making it a high-GL dessert that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume with caution. Sodium content is low (~28 mg per 100 g), making it suitable for those managing hypertension. The peanut filling contributes unsaturated fats beneficial for cardiovascular health. This dish is vegetarian, vegan, dairy-free, and halal, but contains peanuts as a major allergen.
common_user_queries: How many calories in peanut tang yuan?
Is tang yuan suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of glutinous rice balls?
What is in longan red date soup?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry was found for peanut tang yuan in longan red date soup as a composite dish. Nutrient values were estimated by component analysis using HPB Singapore and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for glutinous rice, peanuts, longan, and red dates. GI is an analogous estimate based on glutinous rice-based dessert data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Nutrient values are component-estimated from HPB Singapore and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy sources; no single approved-source entry for this exact composite dish was identified. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based ingredients with no industrial hydrogenation). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — all ingredients are plant-based). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no animal-derived ingredients). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no animal-derived or fortified ingredients). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1.0 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the reference sources. vitamin_b6_mg is -1.0 as it is not reported in the component sources used. folate_mcg_dfe is -1.0 as it is not reported in the component sources used. vitamin_e_mg is -1.0 as it is not reported in the component sources used. selenium_mcg is -1.0 as it is not reported in the component sources used. GI set to 65 (medium) as an analogous estimate for glutinous rice-based filled desserts per University of Sydney GI Database analogous data. GL per serving = (65 x 38.0 g carbohydrate per 220 g serving) / 100 = 24.7. Serving size of 220 g represents 3 rice balls (~120 g) plus soup (~100 g). Peanuts are a declared allergen. This dish is suitable for vegetarians and vegans. Halal status assumed based on plant-only ingredients; no pork or alcohol-derived components identified.
